from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

# Predicate to natural language mapping (constant for efficiency)
PREDICATE_MAPPING = {
    "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
    "deathDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} died on {o}",
    "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
    "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {o}",
    "origin": lambda s, o: f"{s} originates from {o}",
    "nationality": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o}",
    "genre": lambda s, o: f"{s} performs {o}",
    "instrument": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays the {o}",
    "occupation": lambda s, o: f"{s} works as a {o}",
    "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": lambda s, o: f"{s} is associated with {o}",
    "recordLabel": lambda s, o: f"{s} is signed to {o}",
    "activeYearsStartYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} started their career in {o}",
    "activeYearsEndYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} ended their career in {o}",
    "capital": lambda s, o: f"The capital of {s} is {o}",
    "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
    "language": lambda s, o: f"The language of {s} is {o}",
    "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
    "parentCompany": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
    "bandMember": lambda s, o: f"{s} includes member {o}",
    "leader": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
    "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader title of {s} is {o}",
    "background": lambda s, o: f"{s} is a {o}",
    "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"{s} is also known as {o}",
    "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
    "professionalField": lambda s, o: f"{s} works in {o}",
    "musicFusionGenre": lambda s, o: f"{s} fuses {o}",
    "musicSubgenre": lambda s, o: f"{s} is a subgenre of {o}",
    "stylisticOrigin": lambda s, o: f"{s} originates from {o}",
    "derivative": lambda s, o: f"{s} influenced {o}",
    "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
    "governingBody": lambda s, o: f"{s} is governed by {o}",
    "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {o} ethnic group",
    "distributingCompany": lambda s, o: f"{s} is distributed by {o}",
    "training": lambda s, o: f"{s} trained at {o}",
    "populationDensity": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a population density of {o}",
    "meaning": lambda s, o: f"{s} means {o}",
    "longName": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s long name is {o}",
    "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an area of {o}",
    "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded in {o}",
    "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o} above sea level",
    "postalCode": lambda s, o: f"{s} has postal codes {o}",
    "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
    "demonym": lambda s, o: f"{s} residents are called {o}",
    "currency": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses {o}",
    "anthem": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s anthem is {o}",
}

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about an artist (or related entities) into a single,
    fluent English sentence. Handles a variety of predicates and creates complex
    sentences with relative clauses to connect related facts.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group facts by subject to identify main entity and related entities
    subject_facts = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_facts:
            subject_facts[t.subject] = []
        subject_facts[t.subject].append((t.predicate, t.object))

    # Find the main subject (most facts or first one)
    main_subject = max(subject_facts.keys(), key=lambda s: len(subject_facts[s]))

    # Build result parts efficiently
    result_parts = []
    related_entities = []

    # Process main subject facts
    for predicate, obj in subject_facts[main_subject]:
        converter = PREDICATE_MAPPING.get(predicate)
        if converter:
            result_parts.append(converter(main_subject, obj))
        else:
            result_parts.append(f"{main_subject} {predicate} {obj}")

    # Collect related entity facts
    for subject, facts in subject_facts.items():
        if subject != main_subject:
            for predicate, obj in facts:
                converter = PREDICATE_MAPPING.get(predicate)
                if converter:
                    related_entities.append((subject, converter(subject, obj)))
                else:
                    related_entities.append((subject, f"{subject} {predicate} {obj}"))

    if not result_parts:
        return ""

    # Build complex sentence
    if len(result_parts) == 1:
        sentence = result_parts[0]
    else:
        sentence = result_parts[0]
        for phrase in result_parts[1:]:
            sentence += f", {phrase}"

    # Add related entities as relative clauses
    for subj, fact in related_entities:
        if "is in" in fact or "located in" in fact:
            sentence += f", which is in {subj}"
        else:
            sentence += f", where {fact}"

    return sentence + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
